findings.description: Bash invokes `python3 - <<'PYEOF'` heredoc without first sourcing the project venv. Project convention (scripts/write-qa-mode.sh:14-17) sources venv before any python3 heredoc. Stdlib-only usage is not a documented exemption from Standard 3.
findings.recommendation: Add before line 43: `source "${CLAUDE_HOME:-$HOME/.claude}/venv/bin/activate" 2>/dev/null || true` (matching the pattern in scripts/write-qa-mode.sh).

findings.description: Bash invokes `python3 - <<'PYEOF'` heredoc without sourcing the project venv. Same Standard 3 deviation as score-inject.sh:43.

SessionStart canary calls `python3 "${hook}"` directly at three sites (verify_read_size_guard, verify_git_privilege_guard) without venv activation. Standard 3 applies; stdlib-only target is not a documented exemption.
findings.recommendation: Source venv at the top of canary-verify.sh, immediately after `exec >/dev/null` (line 16): `source "${CLAUDE_HOME:-$HOME/.claude}/venv/bin/activate" 2>/dev/null || true`. SessionStart performance impact is negligible; the canary already incurs hook spawn overhead.

findings.description: BA dispatch documentation shows `python3 scripts/blast-radius-tool.py` invocation without venv. Standard 3 applies to script invocation guidance in agent prompts.
findings.recommendation: Change to: `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/blast-radius-tool.py ...` (matching the project convention).

findings.description: QA Phase 6 documentation shows `python3 scripts/blast-radius-tool.py --git-diff ...` without venv activation. Standard 3 applies.
findings.recommendation: Change to: `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/blast-radius-tool.py --git-diff ...`. Note: the very next block (Phase 5 step 1, line ~994) already correctly uses `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 -c ...` — be consistent across the file.

findings.description: Allow rules `Bash(python3 scripts/blast-radius-tool.py:*)` and `Bash(python3 ~/.claude/scripts/blast-radius-tool.py:*)` whitelist the bare-python3 form. This locks in the Standard 3 deviation at the permissions layer.
findings.recommendation: Replace both entries with the venv-prefixed form: `Bash(source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/blast-radius-tool.py:*)` and `Bash(source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python ~/.claude/scripts/blast-radius-tool.py:*)`, matching the existing line-176 close-verdict.py allow pattern.

findings.description: New section uses decimal step numbering: `Sub-step 12.1: Mascot score-update (post-QA, ...)`. Cross-referenced at line 1110 (`Summarise score-update events from Sub-step 12.1`). Standard 4 prohibits decimal step numbering — integers only.
findings.recommendation: Rename to a non-numbered heading such as `**Post-QA Mascot score-update (spec-20260518-225715 §5.1)**:` AND update line 1110 to reference it descriptively (e.g., `Summarise post-QA score-update events ...`). Alternative: promote to `Step 13` and renumber subsequent steps to integers — heavier-touch but preserves the step taxonomy.

findings.description: Four new sites introduce Chinese text outside the documented Standard 6 exemption (which limits verbatim quotes to `docs/dev/ticket-*.md`). Lines 357/655/795 contain `注入位置：角色声明之后、任务指令之前` (verbatim citation of spec-20260518-225715 §5.1 line 113). Line 606 contains `位置：BA → [test-writer] → Dev → QA` (verbatim citation of §5.2 line 167). Pre-existing Chinese at lines 253/1475/1477 is inside `<USER_VERBATIM>` markers and pre-dates this cycle (not introduced_in_diff).
findings.recommendation: Translate the citation tail to English in all four new sites (the spec line numbers already carry the binding citation). Suggested replacement: `per spec 5.1 line 113 (injection position: after role declaration, before task instructions)` and `per spec-20260518-225715 §5.2 line 167 (position: BA → [test-writer] → Dev → QA)`. Preserve the spec line/section numbers as the binding anchor.

findings.description: Six new sites in the close.md AskUserQuestion rating prompt contain Chinese: line 374 cites `仅 CLOSE:YES 后触发，CLOSE:NO 不询问.`; lines 378-383 are the user-facing rating choices (`5★ — 完美`, `4★ — 良好`, `3★ — 一般`, `2★ — 较差`, `1★ — 很差`, `跳过`); line 388 cites `跳过` (skip). The rating choices are user-runtime-visible labels mandated by spec §5.1; the line-374/388 citations are non-runtime documentation prose. Standard 6 has no explicit exemption for user-runtime labels.
findings.recommendation: Two-track fix: (a) line 374 + line 388 citation prose — translate to English citing spec line number as binding anchor (e.g., `spec-20260518-225715 §5.1 line 136: rating prompt fires only after CLOSE:YES, never after CLOSE:NO`). (b) lines 378-383 user-facing rating labels — escalate to maintainer: either translate to English (e.g., `5★ — Perfect`, `4★ — Good`, ...) OR document an explicit Standard 6 exemption for user-runtime locale-bound labels and cite the ticket where the original Chinese is preserved. Style standard currently has no such exemption.

findings.description: score-inject.sh contains pervasive Chinese (rank names 见习学徒/初级工匠/熟练工匠/资深工匠/宗师级, prompt prose 段位/区间/最近事件, user-facing user-rating warning 用户满意是衡量你工作价值的最终标准...). The Python heredoc emits this text as the agent prompt injection per spec §5.1. Spec mandates literal Chinese strings — but Standard 6 has no exemption for spec-mandated runtime strings outside docs/dev/ticket-*.md.
findings.recommendation: Escalate to maintainer: either (a) translate all runtime strings to English (rank names + injection prose + user-rating warning) — preserves Standard 6 strictly; OR (b) add a new explicit Standard 6 exemption to agents/style-inspector.md covering 'spec-mandated runtime prompt-injection strings' with the precondition that the spec preserve the original verbatim, and cite spec-20260518-225715 as the binding source. Without (a) or (b), this file is non-compliant.

findings.description: score-update.sh contains Chinese rank names in the usage message (lines 25-26) and the Python heredoc rank table (lines 97-101, 109, 145). Same exemption-scope concern as score-inject.sh.
findings.recommendation: Same two-track resolution as score-inject.sh: either translate rank names to English (preserve external ID coupling — score-inject.sh, agent-scores.json, downstream readers must agree) or document an explicit Standard 6 exemption covering rank-name strings cited from spec.

findings.description: agent-scores.json stores the `rank` field for all 21 agents using the Chinese names (熟练工匠, 资深工匠). Standard 6 has no exemption for data files containing locale-bound enums. The enum values must agree across score-update.sh, score-inject.sh, and this JSON.
findings.recommendation: If the rank-name strings are translated in score-update.sh / score-inject.sh, regenerate agent-scores.json with the matching English names. If maintainer keeps the Chinese form via an explicit new Standard 6 exemption for spec-mandated rank IDs, this file is exempt-by-extension; document the exemption in agents/style-inspector.md.

findings.description: Two hardcoded `/tmp/` paths in canary-verify.sh. Line 103: `/tmp/canary-oversized-$$.txt` is a real on-disk file (yes >> heredoc destination), so $TMPDIR should be honored. Line 79: `/tmp/canary-safe.txt` lives inside a JSON string sent to pretool-write-guard.sh; the guard does not actually create the file under current behavior, but the path is still hardcoded and would be created if any future guard variant honors the payload.
findings.recommendation: Replace both with `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}` or `mktemp`. For line 103: `local oversized; oversized="$(mktemp -t canary-oversized.XXXXXX)"; trap 'rm -f "$oversized"' EXIT`. For line 79: at minimum use `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/canary-safe.txt` so the synthetic payload reflects the host's tmp policy.

observations.description: Both scripts redefine RANK_BOUNDARIES + a rank-mapping function. score-inject.sh's `rank_and_range` returns name+range; score-update.sh's `rank_for_score` returns name only. The rank boundary list is byte-identical.
observations.recommendation: OBSERVATION_ONLY per codex feedback. The scripts have distinct operations (read-only vs flock-mutating) and distinct permission profiles. A shared scripts/score-lib.py module would be a DRY improvement but not a merge mandate. Defer to a future cleanup cycle.

standards_passed: no-inline-code (Standard 1) — command/agent diffs add documentation prose and Task-tool template blocks, not inline bash/python logic. Verified across ba.md/dev.md/qa.md/test-writer.md/close.md/dev.md additions.

no-hardcoded-values (Standard 2) — scripts accept all environmental values via CLI args (--agent, --event, --scores-file, --hooks-dir, --files, --output, --task-id, --root, --base) with sensible defaults. No new hardcoded URLs/ports/domains introduced in the diff.

meaningful-naming (Standard 5) — no `enhance-*`, `fast-*`, `optimize-*`, `*-v2`, `improved-*`, `better-*` filenames or identifiers in the new files. References to those terms in dev.md/qa.md are NEGATIVE examples (BAD/GOOD pairs).
concise-documentation (Standard 8) — new sections in close.md/dev.md/qa.md/ba.md are functional (rules + procedures + bullets), no marketing language, no story-style prose, no excessive examples.

dangling-references (Standard 10) — all newly-referenced scripts exist on disk: scripts/score-update.sh, scripts/score-inject.sh, scripts/canary-verify.sh, scripts/blast-radius-tool.py. Codex independently confirmed no dangling refs in changed scope.

merge-conflicts (Standard 11) — `~/.claude/scripts/detect-merge-conflicts.sh` output contains zero entries from the changed-files set. Pre-existing markers in INDEX.md / hooks/* are out of changed-files scope.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) returned 7 numbered findings. Material upgrades: (1) reclassified draft findings 1+2 from minor/advisory to critical — 'stdlib-only is not a Standard 3 exemption'; (2) flagged MISSED Standard 3 in canary-verify.sh:95,106,125; (3) flagged finding 3 as UNDER-scoped — also missed Chinese in commands/dev.md:606, commands/close.md:374,378-383,388, and many lines in score-inject.sh/score-update.sh; (4) reclassified draft finding 4 as OBSERVATION_ONLY (different operations, different permission profiles — not a merge mandate); (5) flagged finding 5 as UNDER-scoped — line 79 should also be flagged regardless of JSON-payload context; (6) flagged MISSED Standard 4 violation: commands/dev.md:856 introduces 'Sub-step 12.1' decimal step numbering, cross-referenced at line 1110; (7) confirmed zero dangling refs, zero merge conflicts in changed scope.

findings.issue: Draft findings 1+2 (venv) should be critical, not minor — stdlib-only is not a Standard 3 exemption
findings.proposed_fix: Source venv before Python calls; update settings.json allow rules to match the venv form
findings.rationale: Standard 3 documentation in agents/style-inspector.md says 'All Python script invocations MUST use source venv or source .venv, NOT direct python3' — no stdlib carve-out. Severity escalated.

findings.proposed_fix: Source ${CLAUDE_HOME:-$HOME/.claude}/venv/bin/activate near top, then run Python under that environment
findings.rationale: Three direct python3 invocations in the new SessionStart canary script were missed. Added as separate finding.

findings.issue: Finding 3 under-scoped — also missed Chinese in commands/dev.md:606, commands/close.md:374,378-383,388, and pervasive Chinese in score-inject.sh/score-update.sh/agent-scores.json
findings.proposed_fix: Translate command/script text to English and cite the spec/ticket where the Chinese original is preserved; if exact Chinese runtime labels are required, document a new explicit Standard 6 exemption
findings.rationale: Verified additional Chinese sites by direct grep. Added three new findings (close.md, score-inject.sh, score-update.sh) and one data-file finding (agent-scores.json) with two-track recommendation (translate or document new exemption).

findings.issue: Draft finding 4 (merge similar) is likely a false positive — scripts have distinct operations and permission profiles
findings.proposed_fix: OBSERVATION_ONLY, or reword as 'optionally extract shared rank mapping / consider a single score subcommand script'
findings.rationale: Reclassified from violation to observations[] with classification=observation_only. Acknowledged DRY debt without escalating to mandate.

findings.proposed_fix: Use ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} or mktemp; interpolate both safe and oversized file paths; clean up via trap
findings.rationale: Expanded finding to cover both lines 79 and 103 with the trap-based cleanup pattern. Severity major (rather than critical) reflects that line 79 is currently a synthetic JSON literal that pretool-write-guard.sh does not actually create — but the path is still non-portable.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 4 — commands/dev.md:856 introduces decimal Sub-step 12.1 (cross-referenced at line 1110)
findings.proposed_fix: Rename to a non-numbered heading like 'Mascot score-update (post-QA)' and update the line-1110 reference; alternatively renumber subsequent integers
findings.rationale: Confirmed via direct grep. Added as separate Standard 4 finding.

findings.issue: Dangling refs / merge conflicts — no changed-scope violations found
findings.rationale: Independent codex confirmation aligns with my own check. Recorded as standards_passed entry.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied all 6 blocker/major findings: (1+2) escalated venv findings to critical and added the missed canary-verify.sh sites + the settings.json allow-rule finding; (3) expanded Chinese-text findings to cover close.md user-rating prompt + score-inject.sh + score-update.sh + agent-scores.json with explicit two-track resolution path; (4) reclassified merge-similar-scripts to observations[]; (5) expanded canary-verify.sh hardcoded-path finding to cover line 79; (6) added Standard 4 decimal-step-numbering finding for Sub-step 12.1. Final report has 12 critical + 1 major + 1 observation, all introduced_in_diff.
